step_by_step_thinking:
Coarse tremors of the tongue would generally be indicative of neurological issues related to cerebellar dysfunction or certain systemic diseases like thyrotoxicosis. From the articles referenced, we see that coarse intention tremors of the tongue can be observed in various conditions - parkinsonism, von economo encephalitis, and general paresis (late stage syphilis). However, essential tremor primarily involves higher frequencies (typically around 6-12 Hz) and is unlikely to feature coarse tremors characteristic of those mentioned above.
\boxed{C} Based on the differential diagnosis discussed across several references, particularly noting the descriptions of fine vs. coarse tremors and the specific associations between coarse intention tremors and conditions like parkinsonism and late-stage syphilis, the correct option excluding coarse tremors of the tongue is C: Thyrotoxicosis.
